previousChapter.title: Risks
previousChapter.mission: Rank legal, regulatory, operational, technical, financial, partner, customer, and execution risks with mitigations and kill criteria.
contentRequirements: Severity-ranked risks with likelihood, impact, mitigation maturity, residual exposure, and investment implication.
Regulatory/legal risk — licenses, approvals, litigation, enforcement, IP, privacy, environmental/safety.
Operational risk — supply chain, manufacturing, logistics, reliability, outages, recalls, safety, facilities, labor.
Partner/dependency risk — supplier, distributor, cloud/platform, capital provider, regulator, key customer concentration.
Financial/model risk — burn, capital intensity, credit/loss exposure, fraud, working capital, margin compression.
Mitigations, monitoring indicators, thesis-break triggers, and diligence asks.

plannedTables.name: Regulatory / legal risk register
plannedTables.requirement: rule/license/case, jurisdiction, status, likelihood, severity, mitigation, residual exposure, diligence path; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: Operational / quality / security risk register
plannedTables.requirement: failure mode, likelihood, severity, mitigation maturity, residual exposure, unresolved gap; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: Partner / dependency risk register
plannedTables.requirement: dependency, counterparty, role, concentration, failure scenario, severity, mitigation, residual exposure; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: People / execution risk register
plannedTables.requirement: role/function, dependency or gap, likelihood, severity, mitigation, diligence path; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: Mitigation and kill criteria table
plannedTables.requirement: risk, monitorable trigger, threshold/event, action implication.

plannedFigures.name: Risk heatmap
plannedFigures.requirement: Impact, likelihood, mitigation maturity, or residual severity.

plannedFigures.name: Risk transmission map
plannedFigures.requirement: How risks flow into revenue, customers, margin, financing, operations, and valuation.

plannedFigures.name: Dependency map
plannedFigures.requirement: Critical partners, suppliers, platforms, regulators, facilities, or financing dependencies.

evidenceStrategy: Official — trust/security/privacy/terms/DPA/compliance/status/incident pages, certifications, recall and safety pages.
Primary sources — regulators, court records, clinical/regulatory databases, patent/IP offices, environmental agencies, sanctions/export-control sources.
Independent — adverse reporting, security incident coverage, customer complaints, analyst risk notes, comparable risk factors.
qualityBar: Cite evidence and mitigation for every top risk; never assign severity from intuition.
Define monitorable kill criteria or diligence paths for unresolved risks.
When litigation, regulatory posture, certifications, incidents, safety, or concentration cannot be confirmed, record exact diligence path.
Include thesis-break triggers tied to measurable operating, regulatory, customer, financial, or management events.

chapter.title: Valuation
chapter.mission: Convert evidence into recommendation, confidence, risk rating, valuation stance, scenarios, and diligence asks.
contentRequirements: Investment thesis and anti-thesis tied to market, product, customers, financials, competition, and risks.
Recommendation, confidence, risk rating, valuation stance, and target return/hold/exit where supportable.
Current financing/valuation context, entry discipline, dilution/preference overhang, and whether public evidence supports the price.
Bull/base/bear cases with explicit assumptions, valuation ranges, probability signals, and downside triggers.
Comparable set — public companies, private rounds, M&A, milestone or model-appropriate references.
Exit readiness and final diligence asks plus thesis-break triggers.

plannedTables.name: Recommendation summary table
plannedTables.requirement: recommendation, confidence, risk rating, valuation stance, decision implication.

plannedTables.name: Thesis / anti-thesis table
plannedTables.requirement: argument, what would change the view.

plannedTables.name: Bull / base / bear scenario table
plannedTables.requirement: assumptions, valuation/return logic, key risks, probability signal.

plannedTables.name: Thesis-break and kill triggers table
plannedTables.requirement: trigger, threshold, transmission to thesis, action implication.

plannedTables.name: Final diligence asks table
plannedTables.requirement: topic, missing evidence, why it matters, owner or diligence path.

plannedFigures.name: Valuation sensitivity
plannedFigures.requirement: Sensitivity to revenue, margin, multiple, milestone probability, utilization, or other driver.

plannedFigures.name: Valuation / return range
plannedFigures.requirement: Low/base/high valuation, exit, dilution, or return outcomes with explicit assumptions.

plannedFigures.name: Investment KPIs
plannedFigures.requirement: IC-ready scoring across market, proof, moat, economics, risk, valuation, and evidence quality.

evidenceStrategy: Use prior chapter evidence first, then gather recommendation-critical financing/valuation/comp data directly.
Valuation/financing — latest rounds, valuation marks, cap-table/preference clues, debt/project finance, public/private comps, M&A, secondary marks.
Scenario drivers — growth, margin, retention, unit economics, approvals, milestones, loss rates, multiples, exit window.
Adverse/downside — overvaluation, down-round risk, multiple compression, regulatory blockers, customer concentration, failed commercialization.
qualityBar: Recommendation is price-sensitive and evidence-sensitive, not a generic company-quality score.
Use a valuation method matching business model and stage; show what diligence or price change would move the call.
Do not issue buy unless thesis and anti-thesis are evidence-supported and valuation has sufficient support.
If valuation inputs are missing, prefer research-more or track over false precision.
Include adverse searches on overvaluation, down-round risk, governance, liquidity, or commercialization failure.
